lanes.deferred_reason: QI/device-QI hard-seed and production-ladder evidence has been extracted from the stable core and preserved on research/qi-device-hard-seed until a candidate implementation passes release residual, output, runtime, memory, CPU/GPU parity, and documentation gates.

evidence.claim: Research-lane documentation now defines QI/device-QI as preserved research material rather than stable release evidence.
The core-slim inventory records QI/device-QI artifacts as extract-research material outside stable defaults.
The final plan requires QI/device-QI source, tests, docs, and artifacts to pass strict release gates before returning to stable.

gates: Future QI/device-QI promotion requires compact regenerated artifacts from the candidate branch.
CPU and GPU runs must pass strict true-residual, output-write, runtime, memory, and observable-parity gates.
Stable README/docs must not cite extracted QI/device-QI artifacts as release evidence.
next_actions: Keep QI/device-QI promotion work on research/qi-device-hard-seed or a successor research PR.
Reintroduce only the minimal stable source/tests/docs after regenerated CPU/GPU artifacts pass strict gates.
lanes.second_push_result: QI/device-QI material is preserved outside the stable core; stable release claims no longer depend on hard-seed artifacts that are not shipped.
lanes.title: Production-resolution QI CPU/GPU robustness

evidence.claim: Performance documentation preserves supported PAS memory/runtime routes and separates them from rejected experimental routes.
Matrix-free PAS policy and correction code own byte-budget and fail-fast guards.
PAS matrix-free tests cover fail-fast allocation gates without launching slow production solves.

gates: Keep only PAS routes that preserve true residual and Fortran parity.
Reject candidates that avoid allocation but do not reduce the residual.
CPU and GPU default policy changes require bounded artifacts before README-facing claims.
next_actions: Promote a PAS route only after residual-clean CPU/GPU evidence improves runtime or peak memory on geometry-rich floors.
Keep generated PAS profiling outputs untracked unless they are compact checked evidence.
lanes.second_push_result: PAS memory/runtime evidence remains bounded and claim-scoped; default promotion remains evidence-gated.
lanes.title: PAS and geometry-rich runtime/memory offenders

evidence.claim: Transport-worker parallelism is documented separately from experimental single-case sharding.
Runtime policy code owns worker-count, backend, and claim-scope decisions.
Release-facing GPU transport-worker figure is checked in.

gates: Case-parallel and transport-worker paths must preserve serial output ordering and parity.
Strong-scaling claims require per-worker work large enough to amortize setup.
Single-case multi-GPU remains experimental unless a measured artifact improves wall time and passes deterministic-output parity.
next_actions: Keep release-facing parallel claims scoped to independent transport-worker/case throughput.
Treat single-case sharding as research until deterministic-output and speedup gates pass.
lanes.second_push_result: Release-facing parallelism remains scoped to audited independent work; single-case sharding stays non-release.
lanes.title: Executable parallelism and strong-scaling evidence

evidence.claim: The final plan defines the line-level code-audit and extraction contract for the refactor PR.
The source-map documentation identifies canonical domain owners.
Source-tree consolidation tests enforce the refactor branch structure and extraction rules.

gates: Coverage gains must come from focused module tests, not slow full-solve duplication.
Large driver splits must preserve CLI/API parity and existing solver provenance.
CI-fast checks must remain within the documented time budget.
next_actions: Continue file-complete inventory review and delete/extract research-only stable code.
Raise coverage only after source simplification makes focused tests cheaper.
lanes.second_push_result: The refactor lane remains target-saturated for planning and source-tree guardrails; line/file count reduction continues through the core-slim queues.
lanes.title: Refactor, source map, coverage, and CI readiness

evidence.claim: VMEC/Boozer workflow documentation states the skip-safe workflow and differentiability contract.
JAX geometry adapters isolate optional differentiable geometry handoff utilities.
Workflow tests cover optional backend readiness and no-solve provenance.

gates: End-to-end optimization claims require differentiating through a validated geometry-to-transport scalar.
Boozer and VMEC optional dependencies must fail gracefully when not installed.
Profile-current and ambipolar outputs must pass convergence gates before publication claims.
next_actions: Keep optional VMEC/Boozer dependencies skip-safe in default CI.
Wire only validated kinetic-transport scalar paths into stable optimization docs.
lanes.second_push_result: The scoped geometry-proxy workflow remains evidence-ready; full kinetic gradients require separate validation before release claims.
lanes.title: vmec_jax to booz_xform_jax to sfincs_jax differentiable workflow

evidence.claim: Performance documentation records keep/reject gates for optional JAX-ecosystem solver candidates.
Solver path policy is isolated enough to host future library experiments behind measured residual, runtime, and memory gates.
Research-lane documentation keeps optional solver-library evaluations outside stable dependencies until admitted.

gates: No new JAX ecosystem dependency is promoted unless it reduces runtime or memory on a documented case.
Any replacement solver must preserve autodiff semantics or explicitly remain in the non-differentiable CLI lane.
Hard-coded branch thresholds should move to measured policy gates when evidence exists.
next_actions: Keep optional solver-library adoption studies outside stable examples until a reviewed implementation satisfies stable gates.
Resolve real-SFINCS residual/status blockers before any solver promotion.
lanes.second_push_result: Optional JAX ecosystem solver evaluations remain claim-scoped and are not stable runtime dependencies.
lanes.title: JAX ecosystem solver/preconditioner evaluation

evidence.claim: README-facing Fortran/JAX runtime and active-memory comparison figure is checked in.
Validation manifest records release-ready, bounded-proxy, and deferred lanes explicitly.
Benchmark-doc tests prevent stale benchmark ratio, figure, and scope wording from shipping.

gates: README table and figure must be generated from the same canonical filtered rows.
No jax_error or max_attempts row may appear in public release-facing parity claims.
Low-resolution CI smoke rows must not be promoted into runtime comparison claims.
next_actions: Regenerate final plots only after accepted performance-policy changes alter benchmark data.
Keep README/docs scope language tied to canonical checked benchmark rows.
lanes.second_push_result: Benchmark and docs claim gates remain tied to canonical summary evidence.
lanes.title: Benchmark, parity, README, and documentation publication surface

purpose: Compact research/performance lane tracker for the stable core. Percentages are engineering readiness estimates backed by checked-in evidence, not physics claims by themselves.
second_push_assessment: Stable evidence is now compact and branch-scoped: QI/device-QI campaign artifacts are preserved outside the stable core, PAS/parallel/refactor/VMEC/JAX-ecosystem/benchmark lanes keep small checked evidence, and all future promotion claims must pass strict release gates before returning to README/docs.
